source_file: Phd courses/Information theory for natural scientists/Lecture notes/Chapter 1 (ITFNS).md
stem: For statistically independent subsystems, the entropy of the whole is the product of the entropies of the parts.

Ch.2's core quantitative content (energy-shell (E-eps,E], ensemble-average brackets <f>, entropy S=k ln Omega, hbar-based quantization in Sec 2.4-2.5) cannot currently be verbatim-quoted for quiz generation.
note: 87 of 95 pdftotext unmapped-glyph lines in Chapter 2 (8 are cosmetic running-header noise). Two byte codes are non-injective within this single chapter: 0x3 maps to both epsilon (in '(E - eps, E]') and the ensemble-average angle brackets <f>; 0x7 maps to both an integral sign (Liouville flux equations Sec 2.2) and capital Omega (entropy formula S=k ln Omega). A blanket glyph-map.tsv substitution would be wrong in roughly half its own occurrences -- needs page-by-page visual confirmation against the real PDF per occurrence, not context-based inference. Same non-injective-fallback-byte failure mode already documented from the Ch.1 batch (crop_exercise_screenshot.py's Omega/parenthesis-stroke comment), now confirmed recurring in Ch.2. Current 9-question Ch.2 quiz-draft avoids all flagged lines and is correspondingly skewed toward conceptual/historical prose over the chapter's actual equations.
